records.ety: [OE. flikeren, flekeren, to flutter, AS. flicerian, flicorian, cf. D. flikkeren to sparkle. √84. Cf. Flacker.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Flickered (flk"rd); p. pr. & vb. n. Flickering.]

defs: To flutter; to flap the wings without flying.
quotes: And flickering on her nest made short essays to sing.

defs: To waver unsteadily, like a flame in a current of air, or when about to expire; as, the flickering light.
The act of wavering or of fluttering; fluctuation; sudden and brief increase of brightness; as, the last flicker of the dying flame.
The golden-winged woodpecker (Colaptes aurutus); -- so called from its spring note. Called also yellow-hammer, high-holder, pigeon woodpecker, and yucca.
quotes: The cackle of the flicker among the oaks.
